title: Prepositional phrases
description: Identify prepositional phrases and how to use them!
topic_path: Parts of speech: the preposition and the conjunction

assessment_items.question_content:
**Identify the prepositional phrase.**

>The third little pig built a house of bricks.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Prepositional phrases begin with a preposition and can act as adjectives, nouns, or adverbs. Examples of prepositions include _on, off, to, of, in, out, after, before, under, over, by,_ and _with_.

>*Example:* The box was made **of heavy wood.**

In this example, the prepositional phrase "of heavy wood" acts as an adjective describing the "box".
**"Of bricks"** is the prepositional phrase in this sentence.
